name: Tour of Plana Alta — Circular from Atzeneta del Maestrat
name_original: Tour por la Plana Alta — Circular desde Atzeneta del Maestrat
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Atzeneta del Maestrat traverse a varied landscape at the foot of the Penyagolosa massif, offering diverse terrain from agricultural fields to mountainous areas. The region features numerous mountain passes, including the Coll de la Bandereta and Xodos Pass, known for their consistent gradients and wide asphalt roads. Cyclists encounter views of the Maestrat and Plana Alta, with routes often winding through olive and almond groves. The area's topography provides options for both moderate and challenging road cycling experiences. Best road cycling routes around Atzeneta del Maestrat The most popular road cycling route is El Vidré and Xodos Pass Loop from Atzeneta, a 33.5 miles (53.9 km) trail that takes 3 hours 12 minutes to complete. This route features scenic mountain passes with consistent gradients. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Tour of Plana Alta — Circular from Atzeneta del Maestrat, a difficult 57.8 miles (93.0 km) path. It winds through the diverse landscapes of the Maestrat and Plana Alta regions. Local road cyclists also love the View of Ares del Maestre – Quiet Country Road loop from Atzeneta del Maestrat, a 62.2 miles (100.1 km) trail leading through picturesque mountain passes and expansive views, often completed in about 5 hours 6 minutes. Road cycling around Atzeneta del Maestrat is defined by challenging mountain passes, agricultural fields, and views of the Penyagolosa massif. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate to difficult. The routes in Atzeneta del Maestrat are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from 2 reviews. More than 180 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Atzeneta del Maestrat's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available in Atzeneta del Maestrat?
A: There are over 10 road cycling routes around Atzeneta del Maestrat, offering a variety of experiences for different skill levels. You'll find options ranging from moderate to difficult.

Q: What are the typical difficulty levels for road cycling routes in this region?
A: The road cycling routes in Atzeneta del Maestrat primarily range from moderate to difficult. There are 5 moderate routes and 9 difficult routes listed, featuring significant elevation gains and challenging mountain passes.

Q: Are there any beginner-friendly or easy road cycling routes in Atzeneta del Maestrat?
A: While many routes are challenging, the region does offer options for beginners. For instance, the "Master's Atzeneta" is a 38-kilometer path with a moderate elevation gain, providing comfortable roads through the Maestrat and Plana Alta landscapes. Another accessible option is a route from Tirig, featuring gentle slopes through almond and olive groves.

Q: What kind of terrain and elevation gain can I expect on road cycling routes?
A: Road cycling in Atzeneta del Maestrat features diverse terrain, from agricultural fields with almond and olive trees to mountainous areas at the foot of the Penyagolosa massif. You can expect significant elevation gains, with routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e881931279/el-vidre-and-xodos-pass-loop-from-atzeneta" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Vidré and Xodos Pass Loop from Atzeneta&lt;/a&gt; climbing over 1,300 meters, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24539726" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Ares del Maestre – Quiet Country Road loop from Atzeneta del Maestrat&lt;/a&gt; reaching over 1,600 meters.

Q: Are there challenging road cycling routes for experienced cyclists?
A: Yes, Atzeneta del Maestrat is ideal for experienced cyclists seeking a challenge. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e883506173/tour-of-plana-alta-circular-from-atzeneta-del-maestrat" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tour of Plana Alta — Circular from Atzeneta del Maestrat&lt;/a&gt; cover nearly 93 km with over 1,300 meters of ascent. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38180673" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Atzeneta del Maestrat – Vistabella del Maestrat loop&lt;/a&gt; is another demanding option, featuring over 1,200 meters of elevation gain over 52 km.

Q: What scenic viewpoints or natural features can I expect to see while road cycling?
A: The region offers stunning views of the Maestrat and Plana Alta landscapes. You can cycle through picturesque mountain passes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3410303" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Coll de la Bandereta&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3890802" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Xodos / Chodos Pass (Atzeneta Side)&lt;/a&gt;. The area is also at the foot of the Penyagolosa massif, with the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2839679" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Penyagolosa Summit&lt;/a&gt; offering panoramic vistas. You'll encounter diverse terrain, from agricultural fields to pine forests and rocky landscapes.

Q: Are there any notable landmarks or attractions near the road cycling routes?
A: Beyond the natural beauty, you can explore historical sites. The town of Atzeneta del Maestrat itself has a historic center with structures like the Torre de la Prisión and the Castillo de Atzeneta. Archaeological sites, including an Iberian settlement near the Ermita de San Juan, and caves like Cova dels Aragonesos, add to the area's appeal.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes available?
A: Yes, many of the road cycling routes in Atzeneta del Maestrat are circular. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e881931279/el-vidre-and-xodos-pass-loop-from-atzeneta" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Vidré and Xodos Pass Loop from Atzeneta&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e883506173/tour-of-plana-alta-circular-from-atzeneta-del-maestrat" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tour of Plana Alta — Circular from Atzeneta del Maestrat&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/17509280" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Atzeneta del Maestrat – Atzeneta Pass via Les Useres loop&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about road cycling in Atzeneta del Maestrat?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars from 2 reviews. Cyclists often praise the challenging mountain passes, the diverse landscapes of agricultural fields and mountainous terrain, and the expansive views, particularly of the Penyagolosa massif.

Q: What are some of the prominent mountain passes for road cycling in the area?
A: Atzeneta del Maestrat is known for its scenic mountain passes. Key passes include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3410303" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Coll de la Bandereta&lt;/a&gt;, considered one of the most picturesque in La Plana Alta, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3890802" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Xodos / Chodos Pass (Atzeneta Side)&lt;/a&gt;, which offers a wide asphalt road with consistent gradients. Another notable pass is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3890819" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Coll de la Bassa (Atzeneta Side)&lt;/a&gt;, featuring tree-lined slopes.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Atzeneta del Maestrat?
A: While specific seasonal recommendations are not provided, the diverse landscapes and varied difficulty levels suggest that spring and autumn would offer pleasant temperatures for cycling. Summer might be suitable for early morning or late afternoon rides to avoid the midday heat, especially on more exposed routes.

https://www.dandolotodo09.com/2018/07/puertos-la-bassa-por-atzeneta-apm-15.html

Leaving from Atzeneta in the direction of La Pelejaneta we find the Coll de la Bassa, a tack of only 2 km that culminates in the parched pond at the top that gives the port its name. The road is fairly firm but with little shoulder for cyclists. With a single powerful ramp at 9% maximum slope. A classic on the way back from the Vistabella stage.

This beautiful town in the interior of the Plana Alta is on the list of the 100 most beautiful towns in Spain and it deserves it. With a unique charm, its narrow streets, its houses, the castle and the impressive views make this town a winner.
From the road the views towards the town are very beautiful.
La Plana Alta has several road ports but this one is undoubtedly among the most picturesque. You can reach it either by road or with some variant by gravel track from many directions.
